metadata.description: The 50 most useful Chinese words for everyday couple life — curated from all chapters plus new living essentials

content.name: you
content.side_e: Nǐ jīntiān zěnmeyàng? Nǐ lèi ma?
content.side_f: How are you today? Are you tired?
content.side_g: The most basic pronoun. 你好 is 'hello,' 你呢 is 'and you?/what about you?' 你们 (nǐmen) is the plural 'you all.' Source: Chapter 1.

content.name: good
content.side_e: Hǎo de! Wǒ juéde hěn hǎo.
content.side_f: Okay! I feel great.
content.side_g: Incredibly versatile. 好的 (hǎo de) means 'okay/sure.' 好吃 'delicious,' 好看 'good-looking,' 好玩 'fun.' As an adverb: 好累 (hǎo lèi, 'so tired'). Source: Chapter 1.

content.name: i_me
content.side_e: Wǒ xiǎng chī wàimài. Nǐ ne?
content.side_f: I want to order delivery. What about you?
content.side_g: 我的 (wǒ de) is 'my/mine.' 我们 (wǒmen) is 'we/us.' 给我 (gěi wǒ) is 'give me.' Source: Chapter 1.

content.name: what
content.side_e: Nǐ xiǎng chī shénme? Kàn shénme diànshìjù?
content.side_f: What do you want to eat? What TV show to watch?
content.side_g: Goes where the answer would be — no word order change needed. 什么时候 'when,' 为什么 'why.' Source: Chapter 1.

content.name: she_her
content.side_e: Tā shì wǒ lǎopo. Tā hěn piàoliang.
content.side_f: She's my wife. She's very pretty.
content.side_g: Pronounced the same as 他 (he) — tā. Written differently but sound identical. 她的 (tā de) is 'her/hers.' Source: Chapter 2.

content.name: possessive_particle
content.side_e: Wǒ de māo. Nǐ de kāfēi.
content.side_f: My cat. Your coffee.
content.side_g: The most common Chinese particle. Connects possessor to possessed: 我的 'my,' 你的 'your.' Also marks adjective phrases: 好吃的菜 'delicious food.' Can be dropped between close relations: 我老婆 (no 的 needed). Source: Chapter 2.

content.name: have
content.side_e: Jiā lǐ yǒu māo liáng ma? Méi yǒu le.
content.side_f: Is there cat food at home? There isn't any left.
content.side_g: Negated with 没 (méi), never 不. 有没有？(Yǒu méi yǒu?) is 'do you have?' 没有 (méi yǒu) is 'don't have.' Source: Chapter 2.

content.name: not_have
content.side_e: Wǒ méi chī zǎofàn. Méi shénme shì.
content.side_f: I didn't eat breakfast. It's nothing.
content.side_g: Use 没 for past actions (didn't do) and to negate 有. 没关系 (méi guānxi) means 'no worries/it's fine.' 没问题 (méi wèntí) means 'no problem.' Source: Chapter 2.

content.name: home_family
content.side_e: Wǒ xiǎng huí jiā. Jiā lǐ yǒu māo děng wǒ.
content.side_f: I want to go home. There's a cat at home waiting for me.
content.side_g: 回家 (huí jiā) means 'go home.' 在家 (zài jiā) means 'at home.' 家里 (jiā lǐ) means 'in the house/at home.' Source: Chapter 2.

content.name: and
content.side_e: Wǒ hé lǎopo yìqǐ chī wǎnfàn.
content.side_f: My wife and I eat dinner together.
content.side_g: Connects nouns/pronouns, not clauses. 我和你 'me and you.' For listing: A和B (just two items) or A、B和C (three items, use 、for the first comma). Source: Chapter 2.

content.name: eat
content.side_e: Chī le ma? Xiǎng chī shénme?
content.side_f: Have you eaten? What do you want to eat?
content.side_g: 吃饭 (chīfàn) is 'eat a meal.' 吃了吗？is a casual greeting. 吃什么？is the eternal couple question. Source: Chapter 3.

content.name: like
content.side_e: Wǒ xǐhuan māo. Nǐ xǐhuan shénme?
content.side_f: I like cats. What do you like?
content.side_g: 喜欢 + noun: 喜欢咖啡. 喜欢 + verb: 喜欢跑步. 不喜欢 means 'don't like.' 最喜欢 means 'like the most/favorite.' Source: Chapter 3.

content.name: very
content.side_e: Jīntiān hěn lěng. Wǒ hěn lèi.
content.side_f: It's very cold today. I'm very tired.
content.side_g: Goes before adjectives. In Chinese, you usually need 很 before a standalone adjective — 我很好 (I'm good) not just 我好. 很 + adj is the default/neutral intensity. Source: Chapter 3.

content.name: today
content.side_e: Jīntiān tiānqì hěn hǎo. Jīntiān nǐ máng ma?
content.side_f: The weather is great today. Are you busy today?
content.side_g: Time words go before or after the subject. 今天 pairs with almost everything. Source: Chapter 3.

content.name: tomorrow
content.side_e: Míngtiān wǒ bù shàng bān!
content.side_f: I don't work tomorrow!
content.side_g: 明 means 'bright/next.' 后天 (hòutiān) is 'day after tomorrow.' 昨天 (zuótiān) is 'yesterday.' Source: Chapter 3.

content.name: evening
content.side_e: Wǎnshang wǒmen yìqǐ kàn diànshìjù ba.
content.side_f: Let's watch a TV show together tonight.
content.side_g: 晚上好 (wǎnshang hǎo) is 'good evening.' 今天晚上 or 今晚 means 'tonight.' Pairs naturally with dinner/evening plans. Source: Chapter 3.

content.name: watch_look
content.side_a: To watch; to look at; to read
content.side_e: Wǎnshang kàn shénme? Kàn diànshìjù háishi diànyǐng?
content.side_f: What shall we watch tonight? A TV show or a movie?
content.side_g: Extremely versatile: 看电视 'watch TV,' 看书 'read a book,' 看电影 'watch a movie,' 看手机 'look at your phone.' 看看 is the casual 'take a look.' Source: Chapter 4.

content.name: television
content.side_e: Bié kàn diànshì le, chī fàn ba.
content.side_f: Stop watching TV — let's eat.
content.side_g: 电 means 'electric' and 视 means 'to view.' 看电视 is 'watch TV.' Combined with 剧 to make 电视剧 (TV drama). Source: Chapter 4.

content.name: listen
content.side_e: Wǒ xǐhuan tīng yīnyuè. Nǐ tīng shénme?
content.side_f: I like listening to music. What are you listening to?
content.side_g: 听音乐 'listen to music,' 听说 'heard that/it's said.' 听不懂 (tīng bù dǒng) means 'can't understand (by listening)' — very useful phrase for learners. Source: Chapter 4.

content.name: go
content.side_e: Wǒmen qù sànbù ba! Qù nǎr?
content.side_f: Let's go for a walk! Where to?
content.side_g: 去 + place or 去 + verb. 去吧 (qù ba) means 'let's go.' 不想去 means 'don't want to go.' Source: Chapter 4.

content.name: want_think
content.side_a: To want to; to think; to miss
content.side_e: Wǒ xiǎng hē kāfēi. Nǐ xiǎng chī shénme?
content.side_f: I want to drink coffee. What do you want to eat?
content.side_g: 想 + verb = 'want to.' 想 alone can mean 'miss' — 我想你 (wǒ xiǎng nǐ, 'I miss you'). 想一想 (xiǎng yi xiǎng) means 'let me think.' Source: Chapter 4.

content.name: feel_think
content.side_e: Nǐ juéde zhège diànshìjù zěnmeyàng?
content.side_f: What do you think of this TV show?
content.side_g: For expressing opinions and feelings. 我觉得很好 'I think it's great.' 你觉得呢？'what do you think?' Perfect for couple conversations about food, shows, plans. Source: Chapter 4.

content.name: sleep
content.side_e: Wǒ tài lèi le, xiǎng shuì jiào le.
content.side_f: I'm too tired — I want to sleep now.
content.side_g: 睡 is the verb, 觉 is the object. 睡不着 (shuì bu zháo) means 'can't fall asleep.' 睡懒觉 (shuì lǎn jiào) means 'sleep in/oversleep.' 早点睡 (zǎo diǎn shuì) means 'go to bed early.' Source: Chapter 4.

content.name: go_home
content.side_a: To go home; to return home
content.side_e: Xià bān le, huí jiā ba! Māo zài děng wǒmen.
content.side_f: Work's over — let's go home! The cat is waiting for us.
content.side_g: 回 means 'to return' and 家 means 'home.' 回家了 (huí jiā le) means 'heading home now.' 到家了 (dào jiā le) means 'arrived home.' Source: Chapter 5.

content.name: together
content.side_e: Wǒmen yìqǐ zuò fàn ba! Yìqǐ sànbù ba!
content.side_f: Let's cook together! Let's walk together!
content.side_g: 一起 + verb = 'do something together.' 一起吃饭 'eat together,' 一起看电影 'watch a movie together.' Very warm word for couple activities. Source: Chapter 5.

content.name: at_located
content.side_e: Māo zài nǎr? Zài shāfā shang shuì jiào.
content.side_f: Where's the cat? Sleeping on the couch.
content.side_g: 在 + place = 'at/in.' 在家 'at home,' 在哪儿 'where.' Also for ongoing actions: 在做饭 (currently cooking). Source: Chapter 6.

content.name: want_will
content.side_e: Wǒ yào hē shuǐ. Nǐ yào shénme?
content.side_f: I want water. What do you want?
content.side_g: For immediate wants and needs. 我要这个 'I want this.' 不要 'don't want.' 要不要 'do you want?' Also for future: 我要去跑步 'I'm going to go running.' Source: Chapter 6.

content.name: say_speak
content.side_e: Nǐ shuō shénme? Wǒ méi tīng dǒng.
content.side_f: What did you say? I didn't understand.
content.side_g: 说话 (shuō huà) means 'speak/talk.' 说中文 'speak Chinese.' 怎么说 (zěnme shuō) means 'how do you say...?' Source: Chapter 6.

content.name: give_for
content.side_e: Gěi nǐ kāfēi. Wǒ gěi māo wèi fàn le.
content.side_f: Here's your coffee. I fed the cat.
content.side_g: 给你 (gěi nǐ) means 'here, for you' — said when handing something over. 给我 'give me.' 给猫 'for the cat.' Source: Chapter 6.

content.name: how
content.side_e: Jīntiān zěnmeyàng? Nǐ zěnme zhème lèi?
content.side_f: How was today? How come you're so tired?
content.side_g: 怎么样 'how is it/how about it?' 怎么了 (zěnme le) means 'what's wrong/what happened?' — essential caring phrase. Source: Chapter 7.

content.name: really
content.side_e: Zhēn de ma? Jīntiān zhēn lěng!
content.side_f: Really? It's really cold today!
content.side_g: 真的 (zhēn de) means 'really/truly' and 真的吗 means 'really?' 真好 'really good,' 真好吃 'really delicious.' Source: Chapter 7.

content.name: tired
content.side_e: Jīntiān shàng bān hǎo lèi a. Lèi sǐ le.
content.side_f: Work was exhausting today. Dead tired.
content.side_g: 好累 (hǎo lèi) means 'so tired.' 累死了 (lèi sǐ le) is a dramatic exaggeration meaning 'exhausted to death' — very common and natural. 不累 means 'not tired.' Source: Chapter 8.

content.name: know
content.side_e: Wǒ zhīdao le. Nǐ zhīdao ma?
content.side_f: I know now. Did you know?
content.side_g: 知道 is knowing facts/information. 不知道 (bù zhīdao) means 'don't know' — one of the most useful phrases. Different from 认识 (rènshi, knowing people). Source: Chapter 8.

content.name: tell
content.side_e: Gàosu wǒ, jīntiān shàng bān zěnmeyàng?
content.side_f: Tell me, how was work today?
content.side_g: 告诉 + person + content. 告诉我 'tell me.' 别告诉别人 (bié gàosu biérén) means 'don't tell anyone.' Natural conversation starter after your wife comes home. Source: Chapter 8.

content.name: can_able
content.side_e: Nǐ néng bāng wǒ zuò fàn ma?
content.side_f: Can you help me cook?
content.side_g: 能 is about ability/possibility. 不能 'cannot.' 能不能 'can you or not?' 你能来吗？'can you come?' Source: Chapter 8.

content.name: cat
content.side_e: Māo yòu zài shuì jiào le. Wǒmen de māo hěn kě'ài.
content.side_f: The cat is sleeping again. Our cat is very cute.
content.side_g: 猫咪 (māomī) is the affectionate 'kitty.' 喂猫 means 'feed the cat.' 猫粮 is 'cat food.' Chinese people love talking about cats — 吸猫 (xī māo) is slang for cat cuddling. NEW living word.

content.name: weather
content.side_e: Jīntiān tiānqì zěnmeyàng? Hǎo rè a!
content.side_f: How's the weather today? So hot!
content.side_g: 天气很好 'nice weather.' 天气预报 is 'weather forecast.' Great small talk opener. NEW living word.

content.name: get_off_work
content.side_e: Lǎopo, nǐ xià bān le ma? Wǒ yǐjīng dào jiā le.
content.side_f: Honey, are you off work? I'm already home.
content.side_g: 下班了 means 'done with work.' 你几点下班？'what time do you get off?' Opposite: 上班. NEW living word.

content.name: go_to_work
content.side_e: Míngtiān bù shàng bān, wǒmen qù duànliàn ba!
content.side_f: No work tomorrow — let's go exercise!
content.side_g: 上班族 means 'office worker.' 不上班 means 'day off.' NEW living word.

content.name: wife_casual
content.side_e: Lǎopo, wǒ huí lái le! Nǐ xiǎng chī shénme?
content.side_f: Honey, I'm home! What do you want to eat?
content.side_g: The everyday/affectionate 'wife.' 老公 (lǎogōng) is 'husband.' More intimate than 太太. Chinese people love hearing foreigners say 老婆 naturally. NEW living word.

content.name: cook
content.side_e: Jīntiān wǒ zuò fàn, nǐ xiūxi ba.
content.side_f: I'll cook today — you rest.
content.side_g: 做什么菜？'what are you cooking?' 会做饭 'can cook.' The classic couple negotiation. NEW living word.

content.name: takeout
content.side_e: Lǎn de zuò fàn, diǎn wàimài ba.
content.side_f: Too lazy to cook — let's order delivery.
content.side_g: 点外卖 'order delivery.' 外卖到了 'the delivery arrived.' Food delivery is massive in China — apps like 美团 and 饿了么. NEW living word.

content.name: coffee
content.side_e: Zǎoshang yào hē kāfēi. Gěi nǐ yì bēi.
content.side_f: Need coffee in the morning. Here's a cup for you.
content.side_g: 咖啡店 or 咖啡馆 is 'coffee shop.' 冰咖啡 'iced coffee.' Luckin Coffee is everywhere in China now. NEW living word.

content.name: exercise
content.side_e: Wǒ měitiān dōu duànliàn. Jīntiān pǎo le wǔ gōnglǐ.
content.side_f: I exercise every day. Ran five kilometers today.
content.side_g: 锻炼身体 'exercise the body.' 去锻炼 'go work out.' 健身 is more specifically 'fitness.' NEW living word.

content.name: run
content.side_e: Wǒ qù pǎo bù le! Yì huìr jiù huí lái.
content.side_f: I'm going for a run! I'll be back soon.
content.side_g: 去跑步 'go running.' 跑步机 is 'treadmill.' 公里 (gōnglǐ) is 'kilometer.' NEW living word.

content.name: cold
content.side_e: Wàimiàn hǎo lěng! Duō chuān yìdiǎn.
content.side_f: It's really cold outside! Wear more layers.
content.side_g: Opposite of 热 (hot). 冷死了 'freezing.' 不冷不热 'perfect temperature.' NEW living word.

content.name: happy
content.side_e: Nǐ huí lái le, wǒ hěn kāixīn!
content.side_f: You're home — I'm so happy!
content.side_g: Literally 'open heart.' More warm and colloquial than 高兴. 不开心 'unhappy.' 你开心吗？'are you happy?' NEW living word.

content.name: take_a_walk
content.side_a: To take a walk; to stroll
content.side_e: Chī wán fàn, wǒmen qù sànbù ba.
content.side_f: After dinner, let's go for a walk.
content.side_g: Very common after-dinner activity in China. 去散步 'go for a walk.' 散散步 is the relaxed form. NEW living word.

content.name: bored
content.side_e: Hǎo wúliáo, kàn diànshìjù ba.
content.side_f: So bored — let's watch a TV show.
content.side_g: 太无聊了 'so boring.' Can describe you (bored) or something (boring). Opposite: 有意思 'interesting.' NEW living word.

content.name: tv_show
content.side_e: Nǐ zuìjìn kàn shénme diànshìjù? Tuījiàn yí ge.
content.side_f: What TV shows are you watching lately? Recommend one.
content.side_g: 美剧 'American TV show.' Great conversation topic — Chinese people love discussing dramas. NEW living word.

dialogue_001.title: Coming Home After Work
dialogue_001.description: 小明 comes home after a long day at work. 小美 is happy to see him.

lines.a: 小美，我下班了，回家了！
lines.b: Xiǎoměi, wǒ xiàbān le, huíjiā le!
lines.c: Xiaomei, I'm off work, I'm home!

lines.a: 老婆好开心，你回来了！今天累不累？
lines.b: Lǎopó hǎo kāixīn, nǐ huílái le! Jīntiān lèi bú lèi?
lines.c: I'm so happy, you're back! Are you tired today?

lines.a: 真的很累，今天上班很忙。
lines.b: Zhēn de hěn lèi, jīntiān shàngbān hěn máng.
lines.c: Really tired, today at work was very busy.

lines.a: 我知道，你说的。来，我给你咖啡。
lines.b: Wǒ zhīdào, nǐ shuō de. Lái, wǒ gěi nǐ kāfēi.
lines.c: I know, you told me. Here, I'll give you coffee.

lines.a: 谢谢老婆！有你真好。
lines.b: Xièxie lǎopó! Yǒu nǐ zhēn hǎo.
lines.c: Thanks, wife! Having you is so great.

dialogue_002.title: What's for Dinner?
dialogue_002.description: 小明 and 小美 discuss dinner plans — cook at home or order delivery?

lines.a: 老婆，晚上吃什么？你想做饭吗？
lines.b: Lǎopó, wǎnshang chī shénme? Nǐ xiǎng zuòfàn ma?
lines.c: Wife, what do we eat tonight? Do you want to cook?

lines.a: 我不想做饭，今天太累了。要不要外卖？
lines.b: Wǒ bù xiǎng zuòfàn, jīntiān tài lèi le. Yào bú yào wàimài?
lines.c: I don't want to cook, too tired today. How about delivery?

lines.a: 好，我给你点外卖。你想吃什么？
lines.b: Hǎo, wǒ gěi nǐ diǎn wàimài. Nǐ xiǎng chī shénme?
lines.c: OK, I'll order delivery for you. What do you want to eat?

lines.a: 我没有想法，你说吧。
lines.b: Wǒ méiyǒu xiǎngfǎ, nǐ shuō ba.
lines.c: I have no idea, you decide.

lines.a: 那我们吃火锅吧，好不好？
lines.b: Nà wǒmen chī huǒguō ba, hǎo bù hǎo?
lines.c: Then let's have hotpot, how about it?

lines.a: 好！我很喜欢吃火锅！
lines.b: Hǎo! Wǒ hěn xǐhuān chī huǒguō!
lines.c: Great! I really like eating hotpot!

dialogue_003.title: Morning Routine
dialogue_003.description: 小美 and 小明 get ready for the day, discussing exercise and the weather.

lines.a: 明天你要去跑步吗？
lines.b: Míngtiān nǐ yào qù pǎobù ma?
lines.c: Are you going running tomorrow?

lines.a: 我想去，但是天气很冷。
lines.b: Wǒ xiǎng qù, dànshì tiānqì hěn lěng.
lines.c: I want to go, but the weather is really cold.

lines.a: 没关系，在家锻炼也好。
lines.b: Méi guānxi, zài jiā duànliàn yě hǎo.
lines.c: No problem, exercising at home is fine too.

lines.a: 好，那我先喝咖啡，再去上班。
lines.b: Hǎo, nà wǒ xiān hē kāfēi, zài qù shàngbān.
lines.c: OK, then I'll drink coffee first, then go to work.

lines.a: 你要不要我给你做早饭？
lines.b: Nǐ yào bú yào wǒ gěi nǐ zuò zǎofàn?
lines.c: Do you want me to make you breakfast?

lines.a: 不用了，谢谢老婆！咖啡就好。
lines.b: Bú yòng le, xièxie lǎopó! Kāfēi jiù hǎo.
lines.c: No need, thanks wife! Coffee is enough.

dialogue_004.title: Relaxing at Night
dialogue_004.description: After dinner, 小明 and 小美 relax at home with their cat.

lines.a: 好无聊，晚上看什么？
lines.b: Hǎo wúliáo, wǎnshang kàn shénme?
lines.c: So bored, what should we watch tonight?

lines.a: 我觉得我们可以看电视剧。
lines.b: Wǒ juéde wǒmen kěyǐ kàn diànshìjù.
lines.c: I think we can watch a TV show.

lines.a: 好，你喜欢看什么电视剧？
lines.b: Hǎo, nǐ xǐhuān kàn shénme diànshìjù?
lines.c: OK, what kind of TV show do you like?

lines.a: 你告诉我吧，我没有想法。猫在哪？
lines.b: Nǐ gàosù wǒ ba, wǒ méiyǒu xiǎngfǎ. Māo zài nǎ?
lines.c: You tell me, I have no idea. Where's the cat?

lines.a: 猫在沙发上睡觉呢。
lines.b: Māo zài shāfā shàng shuìjiào ne.
lines.c: The cat is sleeping on the sofa.

lines.a: 她真可爱！好，我们看电视吧。
lines.b: Tā zhēn kě'ài! Hǎo, wǒmen kàn diànshì ba.
lines.c: She's so cute! OK, let's watch TV.

dialogue_005.title: Weekend Together
dialogue_005.description: 小明 and 小美 plan a relaxing weekend outing together.

lines.a: 今天天气真好，我们一起去散步吧！
lines.b: Jīntiān tiānqì zhēn hǎo, wǒmen yìqǐ qù sànbù ba!
lines.c: The weather is so nice today, let's go for a walk together!

lines.a: 好！我很喜欢散步。猫能去吗？
lines.b: Hǎo! Wǒ hěn xǐhuān sànbù. Māo néng qù ma?
lines.c: Great! I really like walks. Can the cat go?

lines.a: 猫不想去，她喜欢在家睡觉。
lines.b: Māo bù xiǎng qù, tā xǐhuān zài jiā shuìjiào.
lines.c: The cat doesn't want to go, she likes sleeping at home.

lines.a: 哈哈，好吧。我们去哪散步？
lines.b: Hāhā, hǎo ba. Wǒmen qù nǎ sànbù?
lines.c: Haha, fine. Where should we walk?

lines.a: 去公园吧，怎么样？
lines.b: Qù gōngyuán ba, zěnmeyàng?
lines.c: Let's go to the park, how about it?

lines.a: 好！和你一起散步，我很开心。
lines.b: Hǎo! Hé nǐ yìqǐ sànbù, wǒ hěn kāixīn.
lines.c: Great! Walking with you together, I'm very happy.
